parsed_review.summary: CP00-392 is a clean descriptor-only bootstrap of the RP13 "Payments AR Accounting Export" program (gates H13/C13, upstream RP12/CP00-391). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review; only a named production_ready_flag and a locally-computed production_ready_candidate exist, with explicit human-final-approval gating and no enterprise-trust claim from local validation. The 150-unit coverage is consistent across the live closeout-pack-plan (CP00-392 at :411, between CP00-391 and CP00-393), manifest, review-baseline, and the payments-core contract: phase split RP13.P00=71 / RP13.P01=79, deliverable distribution {impl 102, contract 6, security_audit 10, ui 14, fixture 3, test 9, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 3} = 150, and 17 descriptor sections with matching per-section row titles. The packages/payments source is pure descriptor generation (frozen objects); a grep for writeFile/child_process/fetch/process.env/object-storage found nothing, and freezeCp392Result hard-codes the no-write/no-runtime/no-leak flags after the object spread so they cannot be overridden. Validators (validatePaymentsCoreCp392Coverage / ...ScopeContractFoundationDescriptor) enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, deny-cross-tenant, no real fixture data, no permission/audit/Hermes-body/raw-payload leaks, no Claude final-approval, and no enterprise-trust claim; tests pin the contract to source constants by deepEqual. Claude stays a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob), not final approver, and Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. CP178+ review-evidence hardening holds: auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid-JSON stdout), validation-report shows no forbidden tools and read-tool inspection mode, and no invalid review attempts are recorded for this pack (the unrelated c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt file is correctly out of scope). Two P3 informational notes only; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. As a read-only reviewer I could not execute the 25 self-attested validation commands and verified equivalent guarantees statically; the human final approver must confirm the post-review deterministic sequence completes before commit.

findings.title: Self-attested command evidence not independently executable under read-only review (expected boundary)
findings.details: command-evidence.json self-attests 25 commands at exit_code 0 (node --check of the four payments sources + validator, node --test of the payments test, rp05-rp13 contract validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validators, npm test/validate/build, and git diff --check), and the pack also declares production_ready=false with a post-review gated command set. As a read-only reviewer limited to Read/Grep/Glob with no Bash, I cannot re-execute these. I instead confirmed equivalent guarantees statically: contracts/payments-core-contract.json mirrors the source constants (program, current_pack binding, cp392_requirements, both no_write_attestation copies, and scope_contract_foundation_descriptor) that the deepEqual assertions in packages/payments/test/model.test.js and scripts/validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs check; the 150-unit coverage math and per-section row requirements are internally consistent across plan (:411), manifest, baseline, and contract; and CP00-392 sits correctly between CP00-391 and CP00-393 (next_subphase RP13.P01.M06.S01) in the live plan. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are correctly not yet present and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending'.
findings.recommendation: Human final approver should confirm the deterministic post-review sequence (normalize -> receipt:validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final closeout validation -> commit) runs and passes before closeout, and must not infer production_ready/enterprise-trust from local validation. No code change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected read-only review boundary, not a pack defect; carry as informational for the human approval step.

findings.title: No-write attestation duplicated across multiple contract keys
findings.details: The identical no-write attestation object appears in several locations in the contract: top-level cp392_no_write_attestation (:335), top-level no_write_attestation (:356), and nested copies inside scope_contract_foundation_descriptor (:2196 and :2316). All copies are content-identical and are each pinned by deepEqual tests against PAYMENTS_CORE_CP392_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, so silent drift would be caught, but the duplication enlarges the maintenance surface for future RP13 packs.
findings.recommendation: Optionally consolidate to a single canonical attestation key referenced by alias in a future pack; no action needed for CP00-392 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Intentional source-mirroring enforced by tests; non-blocking informational observation.
